title: pilosella fennica subsp. exorrhabda
intro: Pilosella fennica subsp. exorrhabda, also known as the Finn's mouse-ear or Finnish mouse-ear, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Pilosella genus, native to Finland. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic, to treat conditions such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been reported to have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, Pilosella fennica subsp. exorrhabda is often used in infusions or decoctions, as well as in tinctures and extracts, to harness its medicinal properties.
